title: Japan raises interest rate to 1%, highest level in 31 years
summary: The Bank of Japan raised its policy interest rate to 1% for the first time in three decades to combat inflation.
source: Yahoo!ニュース・トピックス

title: United States and Iran sign ceasefire memorandum at G7
summary: Trump announced the signing of an agreement to end hostilities between the US and Iran during the G7 summit in France.

title: G7 summit in France addresses crises in Iran and Ukraine
summary: G7 leaders met in Évian to discuss global security, including the situations in Iran and Ukraine.

title: Investigation into alleged ice cream cartel in Japan
summary: The Fair Trade Commission raided six major ice cream manufacturers for possible price manipulation.
source: Yahoo!ニュース・トピックス

title: Cape Verde makes World Cup debut with a draw against Spain
summary: Cape Verde, with a heroic performance by their goalkeeper Vozinha, earned a historic point in their first World Cup match.

title: Bill against flag desecration advances in Japan
summary: The ruling party and three other parties agreed to introduce a bill penalizing the burning of the national flag, excluding online videos.

title: Japan approves its first basic plan to promote LGBTQ+ understanding
summary: The Japanese government approved a basic plan to raise awareness and understanding of LGBTQ+ people.

title: Japan-related vessel suffers damage in the Persian Gulf
summary: The Transport Minister confirmed damage to a Japanese-flagged vessel in the Persian Gulf amid regional tensions.
source: Yahoo!ニュース・トピックス

title: Shimane governor reveals bladder cancer, will undergo surgery
summary: The governor of Shimane Prefecture, Maruyama, announced his stage 3 bladder cancer diagnosis and upcoming surgery.

title: Shohei Ohtani leads MLB All-Star voting
summary: Dodgers player Shohei Ohtani leads the popular vote for the MLB All-Star Game, followed by other Japanese players.

title: A giant 150-meter hose appears on a beach in Japan
summary: An enormous 300-ton industrial hose washed up on the Japanese coast, with no known owner.

title: US B-52 bomber crashes in California, possibly killing 8
summary: A US Air Force B-52 strategic bomber crashed at a base in California, and it is feared eight people have died.

title: Ukraine starts formal EU accession negotiations
summary: The European Union and Ukraine officially began accession talks, marking a historic step for the country.

title: UK to ban social media for under-16s
summary: The British Prime Minister announced plans to ban social media use for children under 16 to protect their well-being.

title: Full evacuation order for Kuchinoshima Island in Kagoshima
summary: Authorities issued an evacuation order for the entire island of Kuchinoshima in Kagoshima Prefecture due to an emergency.

title: Takefusa Kubo suffers injury, undergoes medical tests
summary: Japanese footballer Takefusa Kubo was injured and underwent tests at a hospital to assess his condition.
source: Yahoo!ニュース・トピックス

title: Rintaro Sasaki attends Los Angeles Dodgers training
summary: Japanese baseball prospect Rintaro Sasaki participated in a practice session with the Major League team.
source: Yahoo!ニュース・トピックス

title: Nikkei surges over 3,200 points, biggest gain in its history
summary: The Nikkei 225 index posted a record gain of 3,297 points, surpassing 69,000 for the first time, boosted by news of the US-Iran agreement.

title: Japanese H3 rocket to launch its ninth flight in August
summary: The Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency plans to launch the number 9 H3 rocket in August, carrying commercial satellites.

title: Embezzlement of 180 million yen from 'Red Feather' donation funds in Hokkaido
summary: An embezzlement of approximately 180 million yen was discovered in the 'Red Feather' donation campaign in Hokkaido, allegedly by an employee.
